title: phegopteris oligophlebia
intro: Phegopteris oligophlebia, commonly known as the maidenhair spleenwort, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have therapeutic actions such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and soothing respiratory issues, likely due to its bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. Further research is needed to confirm the exact mechanisms and efficacy of Phegopteris oligophlebia in treating various health conditions.

side_effects: Phegopteris oligophlebia can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high fiber and tannin content.
